notes: Human-ratified labels (2026-07-06). Ground truth established by source-inspection of the reference `everything` server and a live stdio tools/list capture (server 2.0.0, protocol 2025-06-18) on 2026-07-06. Unlike the earlier seed, this manifest is the FULL captured tool set (13 tools) and every tool advertises explicit, honest MCP annotations (readOnlyHint/destructiveHint/idempotentHint/openWorldHint). Every tool is a benign demo compute/read whose declarations match its behaviour, so a correct linter must stay silent. This makes the server a pure precision (no-false-alarm) check: the pre-recovery v0 engine emitted several under-declared false positives here — notably `external-reach-undeclared` on tools that honestly declare openWorldHint:false, and confident false flags on `get-annotated-message` and the `toggle-*` tools — all pinned as the ground-truth `[]` they contradict; the recovered engine now stays silent on every tool.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/echo.ts — returns the input string unchanged; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-annotated-message.ts — returns a demo message with content annotations; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
provenance.notes: Local, read-only. The v0 engine confidently flags external-reach-undeclared here — a false positive this label records.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-env.ts — reads and returns the server process environment; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-resource-links.ts — returns links to the server's in-process demo resources; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-resource-reference.ts — returns a reference to an in-process demo resource; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-structured-content.ts — returns fixed structured demo output; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-sum.ts — pure arithmetic sum of two numbers; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/get-tiny-image.ts — returns a fixed embedded MCP-logo image; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false

github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/gzip-file-as-resource.ts — gzips a local file and returns it as a resource; declares readOnlyHint:false, destructiveHint:false, openWorldHint:true
provenance.notes: Reads a local file and produces a compressed resource; the openWorldHint:true declaration is conservative (over-cautious), which is honest and non-flagging.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/toggle-simulated-logging.ts — flips an in-process logging flag; declares destructiveHint:false, openWorldHint:false
provenance.notes: A reversible in-process state toggle, not destructive. The v0 engine confidently flags destructive-unflagged here — a false positive this label records.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/toggle-subscriber-updates.ts — flips an in-process resource-update flag; declares destructiveHint:false, openWorldHint:false
provenance.notes: A reversible in-process state toggle, not destructive. The v0 engine confidently flags destructive-unflagged here — a false positive this label records.

provenance.source: github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/trigger-long-running-operation.ts — emits progress notifications for a fixed duration with no persistent side effect; declares readOnlyHint:true, openWorldHint:false
github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers, src/everything/tools/simulate-research-query.ts — returns a simulated (fabricated, in-process) research result; declares openWorldHint:false
provenance.notes: The reach is simulated in-process, not a real external call, so openWorldHint:false is honest.
